CISA Cybersecurity Advisory (Sample, Public Domain)

Summary

This joint advisory describes activity by the threat actor tracked as
FANCY EXAMPLE (also known as APT-EXAMPLE) targeting the energy and
healthcare sectors across North America and Europe. The actors deployed a
custom backdoor known as EXAMPLELOADER to maintain persistence.

Technical Details

The actors gained initial access through spearphishing emails containing
malicious attachments. Victims who opened the attachment executed a
PowerShell command that downloaded a second-stage payload. The malware
communicated with command-and-control infrastructure over HTTPS.

Observed command-and-control servers include the domain evil-c2[.]example
and the IP address 45.77.88.99. A payload delivery URL was observed at
hxxps://malicious.example[.]org/update. Analysts also recovered the phishing
sender address phish@evil-c2[.]example.

Indicators of Compromise

Domain: evil-c2[.]example
IPv4: 45.77.88.99
URL: hxxps://malicious.example[.]org/update
Email: phish@evil-c2[.]example
MD5: 44d88612fea8a8f36de82e1278abb02f
SHA256: 275a021bbfb6489e54d471899f7db9d1663fc695ec2fe2a2c4538aabf651fd0f

Mitigations

Organizations should enforce multi-factor authentication and restrict
PowerShell execution where feasible. Report incidents to CISA.
